title: Best study music for long sessions?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Liam. I'm finding it hard to stay focused during my 6-hour study blocks. What kind of music helps you stay in the zone without being distracting?
author: Liam

answers.author: Zeynep
answers.content: Lo-Fi beats are my go-to! There are some great 'study with me' playlists on YouTube that use them.

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: I prefer brown noise or ambient rain sounds. It blocks out everything else perfectly.

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Classical music is also great, but only the ones without intense crescendos!

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: I actually prefer total silence. If it's too quiet, I use a white noise machine.

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: Try 'Video Game Soundtracks'. They are designed to be background music that keeps you engaged!

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: That's a genius idea Sofia, the Skyrim soundtrack is actually amazing for studying.

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: I usually go with Synthwave. It has a steady rhythm that keeps me moving through the material.

answers.author: Hanna
answers.content: Does anyone use nature sounds? Like birds or forest noises?

answers.author: Bence
answers.content: Yes, I use an app called Tide for that. Highly recommend it!

answers.author: Liam
answers.content: Thanks everyone! I'll try the video game soundtracks today. Sounds fun!

title: How to stay motivated during a gap year?
content: Hello! I'm Zalan. I've decided to take a gap year to focus entirely on the IMAT. How do you guys stay motivated when you aren't in a school environment?
author: Zalan

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Create a strict schedule and stick to it! Treat studying like a full-time job.

title: IMAT preparation: Is 4 months enough?
content: Hi, I'm Madison. I'm planning to take the IMAT this year and I'm wondering if starting now (4 months before) is enough time. I'm a quick learner!
author: Madison

answers.author: Noah
answers.content: 4 months is doable if you are consistent. Focus on your weakest areas first!

title: Handling a low practice test score
content: Hi guys, I'm Elif. I just got a really low score on my first mock test. I'm feeling quite discouraged. How do you guys keep going after a bad result?
author: Elif

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Don't sweat it! The first mock is always the hardest. Use it as a roadmap for what to study next.

title: Best ways to review science topics before the exam?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm looking for the most efficient way to review all the biology and chemistry topics in the last month. Any tips?
author: Hanna

title: Active recall vs. Passive studying for IMAT?
content: Hi! I'm Liam. I've heard a lot about active recall. Is it really that much better than just re-reading textbooks? What's your experience?
author: Liam

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: Yes! Re-reading is largely a waste of time. Try blurting or using flashcards instead.

title: Best time of day to take full-length mocks?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Bence. Should I take my mock tests in the morning or afternoon? Does it matter for realistic preparation?
author: Bence

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Try to take them at the exact same time as the real exam! It helps train your brain to be alert during those hours.

title: Staying organized with so many topics?
content: Hello! I'm Zeynep. I'm feeling overwhelmed by the volume of material for the IMAT. How do you guys keep track of what you've covered?
author: Zeynep

title: Dealing with phone distractions while studying?
content: Hi, I'm Jacob. I find myself checking my phone every 10 minutes. How do you stay focused? Any specific apps or methods?
author: Jacob

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: I use 'Forest'. It gamifies staying off your phone and it's actually really effective!

title: Importance of regular breaks during the day?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Elif. I often study for 4 hours straight without a break. Is this okay or should I be pausing more frequently?
author: Elif

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: Definitely pause! Your brain stops absorbing info after about 50-90 minutes. Try the Pomodoro method.

title: Pomodoro technique: 25/5 or 50/10? (Poll)
content: Hi! I'm Liam. Which Pomodoro interval do you guys find most effective for long-term study? I'm currently trying 50/10.
author: Liam

answers.author: Mia
answers.content: I prefer 50/10 because it allows for more 'deep work' sessions. 25 minutes feels too short for complex topics.

answers.author: Luca
answers.content: I'm a 25/5 fan. It keeps me from getting bored or overwhelmed.

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: I usually do 90/15. It matches the university lecture style better.

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: 90 minutes is a lot! I stick to 50/10 personally.

title: Finding student housing in Milan - Tips?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Madison. I'll be moving to Milan next year. Any advice on where to look for housing that isn't insanely expensive?
author: Madison

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Look at areas like Lambrate or Bicocca. They are a bit further out but much more affordable than the center!

title: Student life in Turin: Is it lively?
content: Hello, I'm Finn. I'm considering Turin as my first choice. What's the atmosphere like for international students there?
author: Finn

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: Turin is amazing! Very beautiful, great food, and lots of students. You'll love it.

title: Public transport in Italy: Monthly passes?
content: Hi, I'm Luca. How much do student transport passes usually cost in cities like Milan or Rome? Is it worth it?
author: Luca

title: How much Italian do I really need for med school?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Noah. I'm applying for an English course, but I'm worried about daily life and hospital rounds. How much Italian should I know?
author: Noah

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: For daily life, A2 is enough. But for hospital rounds in year 3, you'll want to be at B1/B2 to communicate with patients!

title: Cost of living in Pavia vs Milan?
content: Hey! I'm Bence. Is Pavia significantly cheaper than Milan? I'm trying to decide between the two for my preference list.
author: Bence

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Yes, Pavia is much cheaper! Rent can be half of what you'd pay in Milan for a similar room.

title: Opening a bank account in Italy as a student
content: Hi everyone, I'm Madison. What's the best bank in Italy for foreigners? Any specific one that has good English support?
author: Madison

title: Dichiarazione di Valore (DOV) vs. CIMEA - Which is better?
content: Hi! I'm Emma. I've heard CIMEA is faster than DOV. Is that true for Canadian students? Which should I apply for?
author: Emma

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: CIMEA is generally faster and easier because it's all online. But check if your specific university accepts it first!

title: Anyone applying for Pavia IMS next year?
content: Hello! I'm Zalan. Pavia is my top choice. Is anyone else here aiming for the 2026 intake? Let's connect!
author: Zalan

title: SAT requirements for private medical schools in Italy?
content: Hi, I'm Jacob. I'm applying to some private schools too. Do they all require the SAT or is there a different test for each one?
author: Jacob

answers.author: Luca
answers.content: Most have their own specific tests (like the Cattolica or Humanitas tests). Very few use the SAT directly for medicine.

title: Applying as non-EU student living in Italy
content: Hi everyone, I'm Zeynep. I'm living in Italy with a residency permit. Do I apply through the non-EU or EU quota? It's a bit confusing.
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: If your permit is for study, you are usually STILL non-EU. If you have a long-term 'Permesso di Soggiorno', you apply as EU. Check the decree carefully!

title: Universitaly portal: Common registration errors
content: Hello, I'm Olivia. I'm having trouble uploading my documents to Universitaly. Is there a size limit for the PDF files?
author: Olivia

title: IMAT results: Wait time for the final ranking?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Luca. How long after the exam is the official ranking released? I've heard it's almost a month!
author: Luca

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Yes, it usually takes about 3-4 weeks for the final ranking to appear. The wait is the worst part!

title: What to do if I miss the first preference?
content: Hey! I'm Liam. If I don't get into my first choice university, how does the 'scrolling' (scorrimenti) process work? Do I just wait?
author: Liam

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Exactly. You'll be assigned or booked for your lower choices. If you wait, you might get into a higher one as people drop out.

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: But be careful! If you are 'assigned', you MUST enroll within 4 days or you lose your spot entirely.

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: This is a great point. I missed my spot last year because I didn't see the email. Don't be like me!

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: Wow, thanks for the warning Sofia. I'll be checking my email every hour once rankings are out.

answers.author: Liam
answers.content: 4 days is not much time at all! Thanks for the heads up.

title: Best tips for biology diagrams?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm struggling to remember all the diagrams for cell respiration and photosynthesis. Any creative ways to learn them?
author: Hanna

title: How many mock tests are 'too many'?
content: Hello! I'm Zeynep. I've been doing one mock test every day. Is that too much? I'm worried about burning out before the real thing.
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: One a day is quite intense! I'd recommend 2-3 a week, and spend the other days reviewing the mistakes you made.

title: Breaking through a study plateau
content: Hi everyone, I'm Luca. I've been stuck at the same mock score for 3 weeks. How do you break through and start improving again?
author: Luca

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: Identify the specific topics you keep missing. Don't just do more mocks—go back to the textbook for those specific areas!

title: Recommended apps for IMAT prep?
content: Hi! I'm Liam. Besides Anki, what other apps do you guys use to stay on top of your studies? Looking for something for scheduling.
author: Liam

title: Logical reasoning help - Syllogisms
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm having trouble with 'No A are B' type questions. Any simple diagrams to help visualize these?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Use Venn diagrams! For 'No A are B', just draw two separate circles that don't touch. It makes the logic much simpler.

title: Staying positive during test week
content: Hi everyone, I'm Bence. The exam is in 7 days and I'm feeling extremely stressed. How do you keep a positive mindset right before the big day?
author: Bence
